entry.title: Purchase agreement field not found into PO DTW , SAP Business One

Nikunj

entry.title: Re: ABAP Cloud Developer Trial 2022 Available Now
entry.content:
I've been seeing these comments about this version being unstable and I just wanted to share that I have been consistently running it on my machine for over 3-4 days at a time and I've yet had an issue where I can't connect to the instance. I do notice from the chart below that memory usage does increase slightly over time. I am running Windows 11 and I have a 64GB memory machine.
*The "blip" in the chart below is when I restarted the container due to a Docker update to v4.30.

entry.title: Show week entered and previous week
entry.content:
Hello experts I need your help with something.

In SAP BO (old version BO but I don't know the version number)

I want the user to enter data for a calendar year and week (0CALWEEK) and see the data for the week entered and the previous week according to the date entered. How can I do this?

You can imagine the sample view of my report as in the picture.

Thanks in advance for your help

entry.title: SLP MDG integration setups
entry.content:
Hi All,

Need your expertise on the below query.

We are working on SLP MDG integration project. We have done the integration setups (DRF config, SOAMANAGER setup...etc).

Now we can send the SLP vendors details to MDG and in MDG we can see the XML is successfully processed but CR is not getting generated. 

Not sure what we are missing? Is it really mandatory we need to implement the BADI "MDG_SE_BP_BULK_REPLRQ_IN".

Really appreciated for your help to fix this.

Regards

Ganesh Devarajan

entry.title: IPS only provision entity, if user logged into another system
entry.content:
Hi all,

I received the requirement to provision users from AzureAD to SAP Enable Now, which should be pretty straight forward as far as I know.
But now I got asked, if it is possible to only provision users to SAP Enable Now, if they, at least once, logged in to SAP Concur. The thought process behind all that is, that we might save some licences, if only the employees, who actually work with SAP Concur are provisioning, instead of every employee of the company.

Now I have two questions.

1. Generally speaking, is it possible, to only provision from a source system to a target system, if they logged into another system, which is neither source nor target?

2. If this is possible, how would I check for this in a condition?

I feel like this won't be possible, because inside the source system transformation, you can only access the user's attributes of AzureAD and i'm pretty sure that there's no attribute to check, if a user has logged into a specific system or not.

Any help would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: How to use different versions of planning in a planni...
entry.content:
1. In a story, add a table widget connected to your planning model.
2. Click the table widget to make sure it's selected, then try to find the 'Version Management' icon in the tool bar.
3. Create a new public from that version management panel.

p.s. you should also have maintain access to that planning model, and a planning license for this to work.

entry.title: Negative Deduction config changes not impacting on existing Quota record
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I need your help!!!

My business requirement it to change Negative deduction field of Annual Quota to -10 days from V_T559L so that Employee can apply for leaves till negative quota balance -10 days. 
I did the change to Neg Ded to field in V_T559L to -10 days effective from 01.06.2024, by delimiting the previous record.

While testing the scenarios I noticed that - 

1. For new hires/next year, it is working fine. I can see -10 days negative deduction in IT2006.

2. For existing employees (for which Annual quota record was already available with previous config of 0 negative deduction in this year 2024), negative deduction -10 days is NOT coming in existing record.

I cannot delete IT2006 annual quota record of all employees as leaves are already taken in this year. Also, cannot change quota end date as system is not allowing.

Could you please help me to solve this? Business wants this new change to be replicated from 01.06.2024 only.

Thank you.

Pratik

entry.title: Re: How to use different versions of planning in a planni...
entry.content: For import jobs, you can map a column from your source file or create a new field using the custom expression editor with a hardcoded version name. If you enable 'Update local members' in the import job settings, and map that new column, the version will be created as well.

entry.content: Definitely a nice example !

Mae

entry.title: Re: Scanning transactions for the warehouse in SAP Fiori / S/4HANA
entry.content: Maybe best to start with something like this... https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-blogs-by-sap/sap-ewm-rfui-screen-using-ewm-mobgui-service-personalize-with-sap-screen/ba-p/13543880

entry.title: How to enable timestamps on document tab for DocuSign integration
entry.content:
According to documentation it is possible to see timestamps for status changes on additional info column on Documents tab:

In addition, in the Documents quote tab, the user can see the document they sent out for signing in the Generated Documents section. Here, the user can view the document along with all the status changes with precise timestamps (in server time), informing them about the progress of the signing process.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_CPQ/f80fbcd4f1c74232839c30ce26886f07/923ac571c47a495eb78068bd0004bfc4.html

But there is no indication how to enable this additional column, how this can be achieved?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to enable timestamps on document tab for DocuSign...
entry.content: To enable the additional info column, there is a dedicated application parameter "Additional Info Visibility", under Shopping Cart and Quotes (in Application Parameters). This parameter needs to be set to true in order to show the column and related information.

entry.title: SAP Digital Manufacturing 24.05 Release Highlights
entry.content:
SAP Digital Manufacturing 24.05 Release is LIVE in Quality spaces, see below some of the latest innovations. Learn more about all 24.05 Release innovations at What's New Viewer.

📌 Installation Point information from Bill of Materials was added to Assemble Components and Component List (via Lists) plugins.

⚠️Log Nonconformances using 3d Model Work Instructions including Component and Installation Points (Reference Designator) information

📦Create custom Handling Units via Production Process

📉New Shift Progress plugin in Line Monitor POD

Learn more here.

🌐  Global Variables in Production Processes

🧮 Real-Time Monitoring View in REO

Learn more here.

🧮 Display the Five Latest Indicator Values in Line Monitor POD

Learn more here.

📬' Manage Mail Server Configurations' app was deprecated and a brand new Destination in SAP Digital Manufacturing BTP Subaccount Cockpit called 'SAP_DM_NOTIFICATIONS_MAIL' was created automatically

📉Assign Nonconformance Codes to OEE Reason Codes in Assign OEE Reason Codes app to report quality losses

Experiencing SAP Digital Manufacturing

You can have a glimpse and experience several aspects of SAP Digital Manufacturing via the Interactive Value Journeys below:

Discrete within SAP Digital Manufacturing and S/4HANA Cloud
Process Industry within SAP Digital Manufacturing and S/4HANA Cloud
Tool Management within SAP Digital Manufacturing
Resource Orchestration within SAP Digital Manufacturing
Production Process Design within SAP Digital Manufacturing
Shop Floor Orchestration with SAP Digital Manufacturing
Time Tracking within SAP Digital Manufacturing
SAP Digital Manufacturing for Insights
Nonconformances Processes within SAP Digital Manufacturing
Discrete Manufacturing in Private Cloud, powered by SAP
Discrete Manufacturing in Public Cloud, powered by SAP
Process Industry Manufacturing with SAP Digital Manufacturing and S/4HANA Cloud, public edition
AI-driven Visual Inspection (BETA) powered by SAP


Do you like this post? Please let me know in the comments section what you think. Any feedback is highly appreciated.

Or, if you have any questions, please check SAP Community Q&A Area, or comment down below.

Thanks,
Manoel Costa

entry.title: Re: MIGO - OBYC transaction key for price differences
entry.content:
Hello @marnun 

Check the documentation to the configuration node: Materials Management -> Valuation and Account Assignment -> Account Determination -> Account Determination Without Wizard -> Configure Automatic Postings. All standard transaction keys and account modifiers are detailed there.

The transaction key most commonly used for price differences is PRD:

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Inventory Accounting with Universal Parallel Accounting in SAP S/4HANA 2022
entry.content:
Hi @sanjay_soni7 

The first transition scenarios were delivered with OP2023. These allow you to activate universal parallel accounting for multiple legal valuations and move from parallel cost of goods manufactured to universal parallel accounting.

Further scenarios to migrate group and/or profit center valuation from a multi-valuation ledger are planned with OP2025.

entry.title: ByD / C4C Built-in Support is down
entry.content:
Currently ByD / C4C Built-in Support is not working.

No new cases created by Customers/Partners through 'Built in Support' appear in the ticketing tool for our Technical Support Engineers to work on. This impacts all priorities.

What we know

  • This stopped working since yesterday 14th of May 08:57 UTC 14.05.2024 for both products ByD / C4C.
  • Customers are receiving the following message: “The receipt of incident 1000000xxx has not yet been confirmed by the provider”
  • All new cases created after 14th of May 08:57 UTC 14.05.2024  are affected.
  • It appears currently that existing cases can be returned and are visible to Support Engineers. 

Current Workaround

Customers & Partners will need to created new cases via the following:

  • SAP for Me
  • Use following link - https://me.sap.com/home

To reach Technical Support please use the above link or the Expert Chat channel.

Alternatively you can contact our CIC (Customer Interaction Center) team for further help:

  • https://me.sap.com/notes/560499
  • support.sap.com/call1sap -> option 2

Root-Cause

  • This is yet to be determined. 
  • The issue is being analyzed by Support, Operations and Development.

Further updates to this blog will be made as new information emerges.

We apologize for this temporary inconvenience

Thanks,

SAP Technical Support

entry.title: Webinar "Ensure Product Compliance with SAP S/4HANA for Product Compliance" on May 23rd
entry.content:
I'd like to draw your attention to this upcoming webinar which our Product Marketing Manager for Product Compliance, Dr. Rüdiger Kreuzholz will hold on May 23rd.

Companies that manufacture or sell products must make sure that their products comply with a growing number of regulations and requirements. To help companies with these challenges, SAP has developed a new product compliance solution called SAP S/4HANA for product compliance. 

This is ideal if you are new to the solution and would like to get insights and ;

  • Hear about SAP’s vision and strategy for SAP S/4HANA for product compliance
  • Get an overview of the solution
  • Catch a glimpse of the solution with a short offline demo

You can register here ! Feel free share with your colleagues and partners, as the webinar is open to all and free-of-charge.

entry.title: Re: SAP Sales Cloud Relationship Intelligence Setup - Sales Cloud Side Configuration
entry.content: Hi, another note is that for "Group Name", the group's email address(ex.ABC@XXX.com) needs to be provided under group section. Thank you.

entry.title: Re: New 1H 2024 SAP Successfactors Time (Tracking) Features
entry.content:
Dear @PetraRana 

we changed the name of the object in scope of the 'monthly time sheets'. We explained it in the WNV too, see Monthly Time Sheets The new name reflects better what it is.

Best regards

Stephanie

entry.title: Vincular OINV y ODLN
entry.content:
Hola!

Quiero generar una query que me devuelta los albanes de un año que se han facturado un año posterior.

He generado el codigo que adjunto pero me mezcla albaranes y facturas. No me muestra la factura correcta para ese albarán.

¿Me puede ayudar alguien con esto? Gracias!!

 

SELECT DISTINCT
    T3."CardCode" AS "Cliente",
    T3."DocNum" AS "NumAlbaran",
    T3."DocEntry" AS "NavegarAlbaran",
    T0."CardCode",
    T0."DocNum" AS "NumFactura",
    T3."DocDate" AS "FechaContabilizacionAlbaran",
    T0."DocDate" AS "FechaContabilizacionFactura"
FROM 
    OINV T0  
INNER JOIN 
    INV1 T1 ON T0."DocEntry" = T1."DocEntry"
LEFT JOIN
    DLN1 T2 ON T1."BaseEntry" = T2."DocEntry" 
LEFT JOIN 
    ODLN T3 ON T1."DocEntry" = T3."DocEntry"
WHERE 
    YEAR(T3."DocDate") = '2023' 
    AND YEAR(T0."DocDate") = '2024'

entry.title: Re: Alert inbox: Sold-to-party not in all monitoring items
entry.content:
Hello, 

The Sold-to-Party is only available for type Technical System and not for other system types as Databases or Instances, this is a limitation which cannot be enhanced due to technical reasons.

Regards

entry.content:

If yes, what is the standard Solution -

Best Regards

Sardar

entry.title: Re: Growth Portfolio and Performance forms synchronization
entry.content: Hi @maseitner, as far as I know, for your first question, you need to define the Attribute/Job mapping in Job Profile Builder to make it appear in a form using "job specific".

entry.title: Re: Purchase agreement field not found into PO DTW , SAP...
entry.content:
Hi,

Have you checked POR1 template for following fields:

AgrNo = Agreement No.

AgrLnNum = Agreement Row Number

Check in DEMO for the field names in POR1 in Query Generator for surety on the fields.

Kr,

Jitin

entry.title: Re: Spock tests in /web/testsrc not executed
entry.content:
This issue was fixed by SAP in the patch 2211.22

See the details under https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_COMMERCE_CLOUD_PUBLIC_CLOUD/75d4c3895cb346008545900bffe851ce/2b10bc71db0242d8a22583ff79b422de.html#fixing-issue-with-spock-tests-not-being-compiled

entry.title: Towards Data-Driven Process Modeling – Enhanced BPMN miner at SAP Signavio Process Intelligence
entry.content:
"Towards Data-Driven Process Modeling" is a series of blog posts with the aim of looking into data-driven approaches in the process modeling space that are transforming the practice. This post is the second in the series, building upon the initial piece on the process modeling lifecycle, which also covers the explanation of various data types that can be leveraged for modeling.

In today's digital age, data is serving as the cornerstone for informed decision-making across industries. Process management is not an exception and leveraging data can unveil invaluable insights into the organizational workflows, shedding light on optimization opportunities. Process modeling stands as one of the core phases of every BPM initiative, and leveraging data whenever possible is key to expediting this process. This approach enables process professionals such as owners, modelers, and analysts to focus more on the details and fine-tuning, rather than starting the modeling process from scratch. With the advent of process mining data, organizations now have access to a wealth of information that can revolutionize their approach to process modeling.  

For many years, different algorithms have been designed to mine BPMN models, yet they often come with challenges such as accuracy, completeness, and speed. Split Miner is a groundbreaking algorithm designed to discover process models from event logs in process mining with elevated accuracy and efficiency. with SAP Signavio Process Intelligence incorporating Split Miner (check the release communication), organizations can harness the potential of their process mining data for modeling like never before. 

Why discover BPMN models with Split Miner? 

Split Miner represents a paradigm shift in process model discovery methodology. Unlike previous algorithms, such as various iterations of the inductive miner, Split Miner excels in accuracy, completeness, and speed. Its innovative approach to filter the directly-follows graph induced by event logs and identify split gateways ensures unparalleled accuracy in process discovery. By striking a balance between fitness, precision, and generalization, Split Miner produces simple process models that accurately capture concurrency and causal relations. 

Moreover, Split Miner's efficiency streamlines the process of deriving BPMN models from process mining data, significantly reducing time-to-insight. By automating the discovery of split gateways and concurrency relations, Split Miner empowers organizations to focus their efforts on analysis and optimization, rather than laborious manual model construction. 

Three key benefits of this updated algorithm in SAP Signavio Process Intelligence are: 

  • Enhanced Accuracy: Split Miner employs a novel approach to filter the directly-follows graph induced by an event log, ensuring unparalleled accuracy in process discovery. 
  • Increased Efficiency: With Split Miner, organizations can expedite the process of deriving BPMN models from their process mining data, significantly reducing time-to-insight. By automating the discovery of split gateways and concurrency relations, Split Miner streamlines the process discovery phase, empowering process professionals to focus their efforts on analysis and optimization. 
  • Improved Collaboration: Split Miner's accuracy and speed enhance collaboration across departments, like IT and business domains. By generating accurate, easy-to-understand business process models, Split Miner enables focus on collaboration over early-stage model fixing. 

How to generate BPMN models in SAP Signavio Process Intelligence? 

In SAP Signavio Process Intelligence, leveraging Split Miner to extract BPMN models from variant explorer is a straightforward process. Follow these steps to export variants as a BPMN model: Select Variants: Begin by selecting one or more variants from the panel on the right within the variant explorer. 

  1. Select Variants: Begin by selecting one or more variants from the panel on the right within the variant explorer component.
  2. Generate BPMN: Once your desired variants are selected, choose "Generate BPMN" to create a BPMN 2.0-compliant model. 
  3. Edit in Process Manager: The SAP Signavio Process Manager editor will automatically open, displaying the generated process model of the selected variant(s). 
  4. Customize: Here, you have the flexibility to customize the process model as needed. You can name the process model, make adjustments, and refine details to ensure accuracy and alignment with organizational requirements. 
  5. Save Changes: Once satisfied with the modifications, simply save the process model within the Process Manager editor. 

By following these simple steps, users can seamlessly harness the power of process model discovery by means of mining BPMN models within SAP Signavio Process Intelligence as well as its integration with SAP Signavio Process Manager editor in order to export variants as BPMN models, fine-tune, and store them in the process repository of SAP Signavio.  

Example BPMN model generated in SAP Signavio Process Intelligence 

Summary and Outlook: 

In conclusion, the introduction of Split Miner within SAP Signavio Process Intelligence signifies a transformative leap in process model discovery methodology. By integrating data-driven modeling, SAP Signavio Process Intelligence empowers organizations to unlock the full potential of their process mining data, revolutionizing the way they approach process modeling (also look at how modeling and mining complement each other). One of the core use cases is what SAP Signavio provides under the umbrella of the Plug and Gain approach with which you can quickly analyze your SAP processes and generate a BPMN model out of the desired variants for further investigations such as comparison with reference models or leveraging it for further analysis with process simulation. 

As we continue to delve deeper into the realm of data-driven modeling, the possibilities for process optimization and business excellence are endless. By leveraging insights from process mining data, organizations can gain a competitive edge, drive innovation, and achieve operational excellence. Stay tuned for further insights and innovations as we continue this exciting journey towards data-driven process modeling.

entry.title: MARD table inconsistency Stock not update as per Report MB52
entry.content: What is the issue if MCHB , MMBE , MB52 Stock showing but Stock not showing in MARD and not able to do any future movement please help.

entry.title: Re: Problem with 2023 year-end in fixed asset accounting
entry.content:
There must be unposted planned depreciation. 

Check the asset involved, you could add a time interval for the periods that have planned depreciation and use depreciation key 0000 (no depreciation), see if the planned values fall off. Then close the fiscal year, then create a new time interval and add the depreciation key you want to use to calculate depreciation for the new year. 

Test is on your quality system first.

Daniel

entry.title: S4/HANA standard PR workflow for PR relase Email notification not recived to 2 level Approval agent
entry.content:
We are using the S4/HANA standard PR workflow for PR release (WS00000038) with 3-level approval. The first person creates and releases the PR. At that time, an email notification is sent to the second approver. However, when the second approval agent releases the PR, the email notification is not received by the third approval agent. I have already checked that email addresses are assigned for all three agents. We send email notifications through the RSWUWFML2 program with the variant below. 

Requisition release Task - TS00007986

Please let me know What Should i further check ?

entry.title: Automate the Interim Account Update rule using Integration Center Job (Time Off)
entry.content:
I would like to introduce the solution to run the interim time account update rule without touching the time-off calendar to carry forward the certain leave balance to next period which is applicable for recurring type time account.

Most of the cases, Customer want to carry forward the certain balance to next period under the recurring - time account type where the booking period is extended. In this case, We have the only option to utilize the interim time account update rule but unfortunately this rule need to be run manually or import the Time Account Change Calendar for some period of time. Many user where failed to execute the time off calendar at the necessary moment and it becomes an additional task for them to track and complete.

I've tried to make the solution of making the automation to run the interim time account update rule with the help of integration center.

Requirements: The Annual Leave 2024 time account has a remaining balance of 30 days. You are permitted to carry over a minimum of 5 days into the 2025 next period (Bookable extended period), which must be used within the first 3 months of the 2025 year. The leave balance is automatically carried forward on the last day of each year.

The following steps has been taken in order to achieve the solution

  • Time Account Type Configuration.
  • Time Account Object-level Configuration
  • Business Rule Configuration.
  • Integration Center - Job Configuration.

Time Account Type Configuration

  • On the time account type configuration page, I'm setting up the Account Booking Offset [Months] as 3 Months. This option will extend the booking validity period from 12 months to 15 months.

Eg:  Account Validity is 01/01/2024 - 12/31/2024 & Booking Validity is 01/01/2024 to 03/31/2025

 Time Account Object-level Configuration

  • I am enabling a custom field within the time account object, which will facilitate the execution of the business rule during the operation of the integration center job.

 Business Rule Configuration

I'm configuring a rule with Scenario: Rules for MDF Based Objects, Base Object as Time Account and Purpose as Evaluate with the following conditions:

If Conditions

  • If the today date matches with run date :12/31/current-year.
  • Time account validity year matches with the current year.
  • If employees have more than five days as leave balance.
  • If the integration center field has the value as "Yes".

Then Conditions

  • Delete the old Interim posting record to prevent the duplication of entries in the time account.
  • Limiting the unused balance to minimum of 5 days.
  • Resetting the integration center field to "Null" to avoid running the rule during the import or manual intervention.

Attach this rule under Configure Object Definitions > Time Account > Save rules.

 Integration Center - Job Configuration

I'm creating the integration center job with following steps:

  • Create > More Integration Types
  • Source Type as "SuccessFactors" and Destination Type as "SuccessFactors".
  • Trigger type as "Scheduled" and Format as "OData V2".
  • Select the Source entities (Object) as "Time Account" and Target entities (Object) as "Time Account".

  •  Map the destination object fields from Source object fields as shown below.

  •  After mapping, you could able to view the preview records page.

  • On the filter page, I am applying a filter for Time Account Type to run for specific account. If you need to execute this rule for a specific year, please set the filter so that 'Account valid from' is on or after January 1, 2024.

  •  I'm scheduling this job to run on the annual basis and at the end of the year (12/31/****).

  •  Save and Continue.

Result

  • The Integration Center job, once executed, will automatically populate the corresponding time account with the interim type record.

Conclusion

After going through this blog, you will understand how to automate the Interim rule and make necessary adjustments as per your needs. I look forward to your opinions and feedback.

  • Read other SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central blog posts and follow  SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central
  • Please follow my profile for future posts narain42
  • It would be appreciated if you could Like and Share your valuable feedback in the comment section👍

entry.title: Re: Catalogs used in Maintenance Notification

Preethi

entry.title: Re: Common questions and scenarios in Higher Duty or Temporary Assignment
entry.content: The following link is a broken link Implementing Higher Duty or Temporary Assignment guide.

entry.title: Re: Inventory Accounting with Universal Parallel Accounting in SAP S/4HANA 2022
entry.content:
Hi @VB2 

Sorry I missed your question. You don't need actual costing to use universal parallel accounting. You can set up your cost estimates with reference to a ledger and calculate WIP and variances for the production orders with reference to a ledger. You only need to use actual costing in those countries with a legal requirement to include all variances in the inventory/COGS valuation.

entry.title: Re: Vincular OINV y ODLN
entry.content:
Hi,

Check this below:

SELECT DISTINCT
    T3."CardCode" AS "Cliente",
    T3."DocNum" AS "NumAlbaran",
    T3."DocEntry" AS "NavegarAlbaran",
    T0."CardCode",
    T0."DocNum" AS "NumFactura",
    T3."DocDate" AS "FechaContabilizacionAlbaran",
    T0."DocDate" AS "FechaContabilizacionFactura"
FROM 
    OINV T0  
INNER JOIN 
    INV1 T1 ON T0."DocEntry" = T1."DocEntry"
LEFT JOIN
    DLN1 T2 ON T1."BaseEntry" = T2."DocEntry" 
LEFT JOIN 
    ODLN T3 ON T2."DocEntry" = T3."DocEntry"
WHERE T1.BaseType = '15' AND
    YEAR(T3."DocDate") = '2023' 
    AND YEAR(T0."DocDate") = '2024'

Kr,

Jitin

entry.title: Re: Response was unexpected text/html Content type error...
entry.content:
Hello Bijaya,
did you ever find the solution to this? I am facing the same issue.
The IDoc receiver adapter error is:

Error Details
com.sap.it.rt.adapter.http.api.exception.HttpResponseException: An internal server error occured: Response was of unexpected text/html ContentType. Incoming portion of HTML stream: (none).
 
thank you, Peter

entry.title: Re: Purchase agreement field not found into PO DTW , SAP...
entry.content:
Hi @jitin_chawla ,

I need it into DTW template. I need to upload the PO via DTW template. 

And I am not able to find out that field into DTW template. IN query generator , POR1 table is showing that field but not into DTW template.

 

Kind regards,

Nikunj

entry.title: ABAP Native SQL not working
entry.content:
I have a requirement to update an external database via ODBC and found that I can native SQL for this.

I also found that I can try out native sql without connection to an external database. If I just use "EXEC SQL" without making a connection, the commands will run on the default connection - that is the SAP database.

Finally I found simple tutorial examples, but I can make none of the examples work.

If I for example run this below code I  get this error:

DATA: BEGIN OF wa,
        matnr TYPE matnr,
      END OF wa.

DATA: exc_ref    TYPE REF TO cx_sy_native_sql_error,
      error_text TYPE string.

PARAMETERS: p_matnr TYPE matnr.

TRY.
    EXEC SQL.
      open dbcur for
      select matnr
        from mara
       where mandt = :sy-mandt
         and matnr = :p_matnr
    ENDEXEC.

    DO.
      exec sql.
      FETCH NEXT dbcur into wa-matnr
      ENDEXEC.
      IF sy-subrc NE 0.
        EXIT.
      ELSE.
        WRITE: / wa-matnr.

      ENDIF.
    ENDDO.

  CATCH cx_sy_native_sql_error INTO exc_ref.
    error_text = exc_ref->get_text( ).
    MESSAGE error_text TYPE 'I'.
ENDTRY.

entry.title: Re: SSO not working in Production but UAT is fine
entry.content:
There are a lot of configuration items that could be gone through that can't be covered in this format.

I have always used the material from 2629070 - How to Securely Integrate BI 4.2 or 4.3 with Windows Active Directory and SSO in Distributed Environments - Best Practices.  There are several troubleshooting suggestions in this KBA that may be helpful.

We are looking into the same integration with KnowBe4. Can you share with me if you managed to make this work?

Thanks,

Ran

About legacy stuff, within newest version we already using 3.1 and can't wait Core/.Net 8 version :), but some of our customers still on the old version unfortunately.

entry.title: SAP BusinessObjects Mobile alternatives
entry.content:
SAP BusinessObjects Mobile alternatives

 

Introduction.

Originally launched in 2011, the SAP BusinessObjects Mobile app has undergone significant evolution to become a relevant application in the mobile business intelligence.

Starting as a tool primarily focused on delivering static reports to mobile devices, it quickly evolved to the growing demand for dynamic and interactive documents. Over the years, it has integrated seamlessly with various SAP Business Intelligence solutions, such as SAP Crystal Reports and SAP BusinessObjects Web Intelligence, enabling users to access critical business reports anytime, anywhere.

With each iteration, the app has introduced new features such as offline access, enhanced security protocols, and support for a wide range of devices and platforms.

Its journey has been marked by a commitment to innovation and customer-centricity, ensuring that it remains pertinent in the BI landscape.

But, today, the SAP BusinessObjects Mobile journey is reaching an end, with the planned release of the future SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025.

We will not deliver the SAP BusinessObjects Mobile application for iOS and Android, with the SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025.

This article details the alternatives. 

 

SAP BusinessObjects Web Intelligence. 

When we delivered the integration of the SAP BusinessObjects Web Intelligence within the SAP BusinessObjects Mobile app, we were looking for the following set of capabilities and use-cases :

  1. Interactive and Ad-Hoc Reporting : Users can access dynamic and interactive documents (dashboards) created using Web Intelligence, directly on their mobile devices, allowing for real-time monitoring of key performance indicators (KPIs) and empowering them to analyze data and generate insights wherever they are.
  2. Offline Access: The Mobile app supports offline access to Web Intelligence documents, allowing users to view and interact with reports even when they are not connected to the internet.
  3. Drill-Down and Drill-Through: Users can drill down into detailed data or drill through to related reports seamlessly within the Mobile app, facilitating deeper analysis and exploration of data, when they are connected to the internet.
  4. Annotation and Collaboration: The app enables users to annotate and share Web Intelligence reports with colleagues directly from their mobile devices, fostering collaboration and decision-making.
  5. Scheduled Report Delivery: Users can schedule Web Intelligence reports for automatic delivery to their mobile devices, ensuring that they have access to the latest version without manual intervention.
  6. Security and Access Control: The Mobile app integrates with SAP BusinessObjects security features, ensuring that access to Web Intelligence documents is governed by role-based permissions and data encryption protocols, safeguarding sensitive information.
  7. Customization and Personalization: Users can customize the display and layout of Web Intelligence reports on their mobile devices to suit their preferences, enhancing the overall user experience.

With the planned release of the SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025, and the recent innovations delivered in the SAP BusinessObjects BI4.3 SP03, the alternatives to the BI Mobile app and its use-cases are the following ones : 

  1. Use the OpenDocument URL technology to access any Web Intelligence document from a Mobile device, using the device browser and the new WebI responsive interface delivered starting BI 4.3 SP3.
  2. Use the connected mode to access your favorite WebI documents..
    For an offline access use-case, the alternative resides into the planned "Web Intelligence Offline Artefact" in the BI 2025.  
  3.  Drill-Down and Drill-Through are supported through the device browser in connected mode. 
  4. Annotation and Collaboration are supported with the browser access and the Web Intelligence responsive interface. 
  5. Scheduled Report to the Mobile device is no longer supported.
    As an alternative, you can copy/paste the OpenDocument URL on your device, and add the parameter to open the latest instance to access up to date information. 
  6. Security and Access Control : with the browser access, the same security and protocols applies like on your laptop.
  7. Customization and Personalization : all your existing Web Intelligence documents are potentially accessible on your Mobile now, as is.
    But, with the BI 2025, we will introduce the feature "Custom Canvas" that will enable the creation of Reports that will match exactly the screen of your devices. 

 

Crystal Reports. 

Experience the power of SAP Crystal Reports on the go within BI2025, where you can effortlessly view and interact with your reports using the web browser on your mobile devices. Access is simplified through two user-friendly ways:

  • BI Launch Pad: Dive into your Crystal Reports with ease through the intuitive BI Launch Pad interface.
    Whether you're analyzing data, generating insights, or sharing information, BI Launch Pad ensures a seamless and efficient experience which will be enhanced for mobile usage.
  • Open Document: Instantly access your reports on the move with Open Document functionality.
    Simply click a link or scan a QR code, and gain immediate access to the valuable insights contained within your Crystal Reports, all from the convenience of your mobile browser.

With these two robust methods at your fingertips, you are able to stay connected, productive and engage with your data anytime, anywhere.

entry.title: Re: Basis - Bulk user query based on T-Code
entry.content:
Hello,

Users are assigned roles and the roles themselves have tcodes and authorization profiles that define that they can do. You can view the assignments with tcode SUIM-->Roles-->Roles by Complex Selection Criteria-->Roles by Complex Selection Criteria OR transaction S_BCE_68001425. You add a value for the user under "with valid assignment of user" and you can add multiple users or simply leave it checked but blank and execute. This will give a list of roles. You can click on each one and click on "Transaction Assignments".

Additionally, you can also use table AGR_USERS in SE16N and enter a group of users and this will display all the roles each user has. You can then get the list of roles from this table and open a new session for SE16N table AGR_1251 and enter the list of roles and object S_TCODE and this will give you all t-codes per role that each user has. Hope this helps.

Elizabeth

entry.title: Re: Secondary cost elements for Internal Project Settlements
entry.content:

You could choose to use category 22 cost element.

We're having a similar issue with my team and tried your solution but it didn't seems to work.

@former_member751019 @Jitendra_Kansal

entry.title: Migration of Rules from BRMS to SAP Build Process Automation Manage Rules Project or Decision
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am looking for best practise for migration of rules from BRMS to SAP BPA .

As I see there are couple of options  

a. Decision b. Manage Rule Projects

Here are the scenarios and which tools should be the best practise ?

  1.  We have few  projects which are SAP IRPA we are migrating to BPA . IRPA projects called BRMS via API . What would be a better approach here to import BRMS rules into MRP and use the API call to invoke the rules or convert the rules to decision or restructure and design the whole automation in order to use decisions ?
  2. In case there are CAPM or other application which are consuming BRMS via API what would be a better approach Decision or Manage Rule Project

Few generic questions 

  1. Rules in Manage rule projects can be transported across tenant via CTMS
  2. How do we achieve maintainability of decision in production tenant
  3. In case of Manage Rule Project we see + ( Create Rule) button is disabled so incase if new rule need to be added how can we achieve the same ?

Also best practise document or link for Manage Rule Projects and Decision will be helpful.

 

Please guide.

 

Regards,

Gaurang

entry.title: SAP Opentext VIM issue of reminder email not sent to approver for resubmittted invoice
entry.content:
Hi experts!

I have an issue with rejected documents which are resubmitted to approval process again. The reminder notification is not geneated, thus no email is sent to the approver to notify him about document awaiting for approval.

This is a No PO processing document : When approver rejects the document, it is sent back to the AP_PROCESSOR workplace. 

Once AP_PROCESSOR makes the changes on the document, he resubmit it for Start approval (Option Start approval Process again).  But no reminder email is generated even though a work item exists for the document.

Does someone meet the same issue and how do you fix it?


Thanks in advance for your feedback,

Tran Thi

entry.title: Re: Identity Access Management (IAM) Reference Architectures 2024
entry.content:
Hi G,

good catch - let's comment also directly in the Cloud Identity documentation to phrase this more detailed because in the BTP Best Practice Guide is already a hint to the go-to-reference architecture.

From security perspective IAM tiers should be decoupled from the Business Application tiers.

What do I mean with it? (I guess we will write an own blog post for it but in short)

Best case example is a developer which uses the (prod) identity, maintained by the (prod) HXM and the (prod) Cloud Identity tenant but was provisioned with access to a (test) business application. This developer authenticates via the (prod) Cloud Identity Services (IAS) because otherwise testing activities in the (test) Cloud Identity Services (IAS) might block this developer from working in the (test) business applications. From Access Governance perspective it is also important to keep track about the access of this developer in all business applications via the (prod) IAM tools.

To support local test users and test users for integration tests customer specific trade-off decisions need to be done e.g. additional Cloud Identity tenants for SAP Success Factors which acts as source & target in certain scenarios or additional business application tenants to only test IAM integration tests.

There are also good reasons to connect such non-prod business applications to the (test) Cloud Identity Services and maintain the identity lifecycle remotely from the (prod) Cloud Identity Services and for the end-to-end Identity Lifecycle tests also test-identities feeds from HXM / IDM (test) upstream to the (test) business apps downstream.

We are working on such topics and will aggregate the content in additional reference architectures. For now please use the BTP Best Practices and the colleagues will link/update the Cloud Identity content with it.

Cheers,
Gunnar

entry.title: Asset accounting
entry.content: What is the difference between posting asset acquisition on a net basis (discounts deducted), or posting as a gross amount (discounts deducted only on payment)?

entry.title: How to mass change user timezone settings in S/4HANA Cloud?
entry.content:
I am using manage workforce app for mass users creation with import method below

  • Worker and Work Agreement (Basic Import)

After user creation, Users time zone are UTC by default.

I want to change time zone to "Asia, Kolkata" for all users.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to mass change user timezone settings in S/4HANA...
entry.content:
It can be achieved via app "Maintain Business Users".

  • Navigate to app "Maintain Business Users"
  • Select (check) the desired users
  • Click "Mass change" .
  • In the popup, ensure that "User Data" is selected for the field "Area" For "Attributes", select the desired property to change. For this scenario, "Time Zone".
  • Review and confirm.

entry.title: Do escalated workflows honor auto-delegation settings?
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

If a workflow is escalated to an user who has set up auto-delegation, will the escalation be redirected to the delegatee? It doesn't appear to be the case, but I wanted to confirm.

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: SAP EWM Batch Management, Decentralised EWM
entry.content:
Hello,

Please implement a badi /SCWM/EX_DLV_BATCH_NUMBERING which allows to modify batch number based on certain input criteria, IV_PRODUCTNO,IV_ITEMID, IV_DOCID. In the badi, there is an exporting parameter EV_BATCHNO which you can manipulate as your requirement.

br

Shailesh

entry.title: External HU numbering in COP21
entry.content: Is it possible to generate external HU numbers (alphanumeric) in COP21 in an HUM-IM storage location?

entry.title: Manage comments created in Lumira Applications by the user
entry.content:
Hi.

In our Lumira applications, we recently added the feature so the user can create comments. The comments are stored on the audit database.

Now, different users created a lot of test comments in different Lumira Applications. And now we have the need to maintain these comments, for example, edit typos and delete unwanted comments.

The idea was to create a new Lumira applicaton for managing the comments.

Unfortunately, when using the method

COMMENTS.getComments();

in the Lumira applicaton for managing the comments, no comments are returned. In the CMC itself there's also no option to read/edit/delete the comments created in the Lumira Applications.

How is possible to do this?

Thx!

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

entry.title: Re: Payment stuck in Payment Medium Created status in Qua...
entry.content:
Follow the resolution steps given in the SAP KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/3468342/E 

 

Kind regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: Configuration: SAP Business Network SSO with SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Authentication
entry.content:
See as well: 

  • Summary: SAP Ariba SSO with SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Authentication
  • Overview: SAP Ariba SSO with SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Authentication

Table of Contents:

  • SAP Business Network Single Sign-On (SSO) Overview
    • SAP Business Network Access
      • SAP Business Network Credentials Access (SSO Disabled)
      • SAP Business Network SSO Access
      • SAP Business Network SSO Access via IdP-Initiated URL
      • SAP Business Network SSO & Credentials Access
  • SAP Business Network Single Sign-On (SSO) Setup
    • SAP IAS SAML Metadata Retrieval
    • SAP Business Network SAML Metadata Retrieval
    • SAP IAS SAML Authentication Configuration
    • SAP Business Network User Configuration
    • SAP Business Network SAML Authentication Configuration (non-self-service)

SAP Business Network Single Sign-On Overview

Note: Single Sign-On (SSO) is supported only for SAP Business Network Buyer access (SAP Business Network Supplier access is not supported).

SAP Business Network Access

SAP Business Network can be accessed via https://service.ariba.com 

 To access SAP Business Network as Buyer, navigate to [Buyer] button or directly navigate to https://service.ariba.com/Buyer.aw 

 

To access the SAP Business Network as Buyer, enter the Buyer user login name and hit [Next]. Based on the SAP Business Network Buyer account SSO setup, one of below login screens will appear.

SAP Business Network Credentials Access (SSO Disabled)

In case SAP Business Network Single Sign-on (SSO) for Buyer account is disabled, login screen to enter the SAP Business Network password is displayed.

SAP Business Network SSO Access

In case SAP Business Network Single Sign-on (SSO) for Buyer account is enabled with SAP Business Network credentials access is disabled, configured SSO login screen to enter the SSO credentials is displayed (e.g. the SAP IAS login screen in case the SSO is configured with SAP IAS).

SAP Business Network SSO Access via IdP-Initiated URL

With the assumption that the SAP Business Network SSO is enabled with SAP IAS as per this blog instructions, you can access SAP Business Network instead of https://service.ariba.com link, by following the IdP-Initiated URL:

  • https://<SAP IAS tenant  id>.accounts.ondemand.com/saml2/idp/sso?sp=ANxxxxxxxxxxx-T&index=1

Using this approach, you can skip one step, which is providing the SAP Business Network user id and login directly within your SAP IAS login credentials.

SAP Business Network SSO & Credentials Access

In case SAP Business Network Single Sign-on (SSO) for Buyer account is enabled together with SAP Business Network credentials access, login screen to enter the SAP Business Network password or [Sign in with SSO] choice is displayed.

SAP Business Network Single Sign-On (SSO) Setup

SAP IAS SAML Metadata Retrieval

To retrieve SAML Metadata from SAP IAS:

  • enter the below SAP IAS URL into browser:
    https://<SAP IAS tenant  id>.accounts.ondemand.com/saml2/metadata?action=download
  •  store the downloaded SAP IAS Metadata File

SAP Business Network SAML Metadata Retrieval

Note: Retrieval of SAP Business Network SAML Metadata is not self-service and needs to be requested via case opened against SBN-AN-LOG component. This blog instructions are bypassing the SAP Business Network SAML Metadata retrieval and instead it provides manual steps of setting the SAP Business Network Buyer Account in SAP IAS.

To retrieve the SAP Business Network signing certificate navigate to https://support.ariba.com/item/view/192337 and download Current Certificate – RSA certificate for service.ariba.com and store it as SAP Business Network Signing Certificate File

SAP IAS SAML Authentication Configuration

Prerequisites:

  • SAP IAS user added as Administrator to SAP IAS (Users & Authorizations -> Administrators -> [Add])
  • Retrieve SAP Business Network Buyer Account ANId (e.g. ANxxxxxxxxxxx-T)
    • Replace xxxxxxxxxxx with your SAP Business Network Buyer Account AN Id

Note: SAP Business Network SSO setup requires the IdP-Initiated SSO to be enabled in SAP IAS.

To enable IdP-Initiated SSO in SAP IAS:

  • enter the SAP IAS Administration Console via https://<SAP IAS tenant  id>.accounts.ondemand.com/admin
  • navigate to Application & Resources -> 
  • Application & Resources -> Tenant Settings -> Single Sign-On -> IdP-Initiated SSO

 

 To configure SAP IAS SAML Authentication with SAP Business Network:

  • enter the SAP IAS Administration Console via https://<SAP IAS tenant  id>.accounts.ondemand.com/admin
  • navigate to Application & Resources -> Application -> [Create] to create Application for SAP Ariba as Service Provider (SP)
    • Enter the Display Name, choose SAP Ariba solution as Type, SAML 2.0 as Protocol Type and hit [Create]

 

  • navigate to SAML 2.0 Configuration and enter:
    • Name <SAP Business Network Buyer Account AN Id> (e.g. ANxxxxxxxxxxx-T)
    • Assertion Consumer Service Endpoints
      • Index 0: https://service.ariba.com/Buyer.aw/ad/relaySAML?anid=ANxxxxxxxxxxx-T
      • Index 1: https://service.ariba.com/Buyer.aw/ad/remoteLogin?anid=ANxxxxxxxxxxx-T
    • Signing Certificate
      • upload the downloaded SAP Business Network Signing Certificate File
    • Signing Options
      • Sign authentication responses -> true

 

  • hit [Save]

  •  navigate to Subject Name Identifier and set the Primary Attribute Value to Login Name and hit [Save]

Note: Subject Name Identifier setting can vary based on the customer user setup in SAP IAS. The property chosen in the Subject Name Identifier in the user profile in SAP IAS shall hold the very same value as the Corporate Username of the user in SAP Business Network.

  • navigate to Users & Authorizations -> User Management -> and specific user SAP IAS Login Name needs to match user SAP Ariba UniqueName

 SAP IAS User Profile:

 SAP Business Network User Profile:

 

SAP Business Network User Configuration

Note: 

  • Navigate to SAP Business Network Buyer account Settings -> Users -> Manage Users

  • Actions -> Edit -> Corporate Username

 

SAP Business Network SAML Authentication Configuration (non-self-service)

Note: SAP Business Network Single Sign-On (SSO) configuration is not self-service and needs to be requested as per the instructions below.

Create case against SBN-AN-LOG component providing below details:

  • SAP Business Network Buyer Account Id (e.g. ANxxxxxxxxxxx-T)
  • SAP IAS URL https://<SAP IAS tenant  id>.accounts.ondemand.com
  • SAP IAS Metadata File
  • Disable SAP Business Network Login: Yes/No
  • SAP IAS Login Id for SAP Business Network Admin User (e.g. I0****6 - this needs to match the SAP IAS user profile property value, which is set as Subject Name Identifier)
    • SAP Business Network Admin User is not maintained in the list of the users in SAP Business Network and thus his Corporate Username cannot be set, therefore the SAP IAS Login Id shall be setup extra and passed as this setting

SAP personnel as part of the case execution will apply below setting in the SAP Business Network:

  • Assertion Issuer: SAP IAS URL (e.g. https://<SAP IAS tenant  id>.accounts.ondemand.com
  • Corporate User ID: SAP IAS Login Id for SAP Business Network Admin User (e.g. I0****6)
  • Site Minder Affliate Name: SAP Business Network Buyer Account Id (e.g. ANxxxxxxxxxxx-T)
  • Get Assertion Service URL: https://<SAP IAS tenant  id>.accounts.ondemand.com/saml2/idp/sso?sp=<SAP Business Network Buyer Account Id>&index=1 (e.g. https://<SAP IAS tenant  id>.accounts.ondemand.com/saml2/idp/sso?sp=ANxxxxxxxxxxx-T&index=1 
  • Portal Query URL: 
  • https://<SAP IAS tenant  id>.accounts.ondemand.com/saml2/idp/sso?sp=<SAP Business Network Buyer Account Id>&index=1 (e.g. https://<SAP IAS tenant  id>.accounts.ondemand.com/saml2/idp/sso?sp=ANxxxxxxxxxxx-T&index=1 
  • Portal Login URL: 
  • https://<SAP IAS tenant  id>.accounts.ondemand.com/saml2/idp/sso?sp=<SAP Business Network Buyer Account Id>&index=1 (e.g. https://<SAP IAS tenant  id>.accounts.ondemand.com/saml2/idp/sso?sp=ANxxxxxxxxxxx-T&index=1 
  • Disable Manual Logon to AN: Disable SAP Business Network Login
  • Certificate Store for Site Minder SSL: SAP IAS Metadata File

 

Once the instructions in opened case are executed, you have successfully configured the Single Sign-On (SSO) between SAP Business Network as Service Provider (SP) and SAP IAS as Identity Provider (IdP)!

See as well: 

  • Summary: SAP Ariba SSO with SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Authentication
  • Overview: SAP Ariba SSO with SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Authentication

entry.title: Re: How-to use CDS 7 and Node.js 18 required by CAP in BAS ?
entry.content:
Anyone reading this thread, please note that the Node version in BAS is being continuously updated.
Today the Node version is 20.
Also, as of November 2023 BAS also support changing the runtime version of Node. See more here.

entry.title: Re: Upload Excel using SAP RAP Only
entry.content: My comment was primarily about selling one thing in the title and then delivering something completely different. Content that is inferior and does not fulfil the promise of the headline fits the definition of clickbait, and is generally considered bad form. Pointing out that the advertised functionality is left for the reader to figure out suggests that it was intentionally misleading. "Upload file using RAP only" would be more honest and still as interesting.

entry.title: Where can I change the Comany name in my P-User account?
entry.content:
In my P-User account under account.sap.com I don't see any possibility to change my current company name.

Can someone give me a hint, where this can be done?

Thanks and cheers,
Uwe

entry.title: Re: Transport request in npm deploy is blank: Error is 400
entry.content:
Hi @SBasu1234,

please have a look at note https://me.sap.com/notes/3424818. This should answer your question.

Best regards,
Kai

entry.title: cancel the moved request
entry.content:
Hello,
My question is, can a request moved to the BIQ system be rolled back? Can the request be withdrawn?

entry.title: BW HANA Sizing - Results interpretation /SDF/HANA_BW_SIZING
entry.content:
How should the results of the report /SDF/HANA_BW_SIZING be interpreted? is the recommended physical memory per node the final "HANA DB size" also displayed in the Readiness check report? It is very confusing as the sum of the memory 838 GB + data 1271 GB + log 512 GB disk size, is not equal to the recommended physical memory per node (1536 GB). 

Thanks for any clarification.

entry.title: Re: ABAP Cloud Developer Trial 2022 Available Now
entry.content:
Hi @julieplummer20 , 

First off, thanks for the new version, much appreciated. 

There was talk of providing an updated SAP GUI to go with 2022, is this still in the works? I'm having several issues with the frontend.

As an aside, I don't have any of the login issues as discussed here. I run it on a NAS with static IPs for both the NAS and the container, and it runs happily for days on end.

Thanks & regards,
Mike

entry.title: Re: error w while trying to create a blog
entry.content: thanks for the link

entry.title: Re: How to check your SAP Queries for SAP S/4HANA readiness
entry.content:
Hi @sandipjana ,

actually the check does not inspect if the SAP standard table still exists in S/4HANA, it rather inspects if there is an access to the SAP standard table, which was simplified in S/4HANA. Then you can follow the simplification item to solve the issue with the fields.

Kind regards,
Olga.

entry.title: Re: I have a Enhancement for F3302 App
entry.content:
Version: 1.87.1
Commit: 
Date: 2024-04-04T07:11:26.010Z
Browser: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/124.0.0.0 Safari/537.36

i was trying to make other solution with create an Adaptation Project in SAP Business Application Studio and then implement the extension using Visual Editor. I can see the filter Account Assigment Category.

1. add filter account

But with this filter the customer want to implement the logic 

BSEG- BLART=’WE’ or BSEG- BLART=’WA’
&& BSEG-BSCHL≠’96’
&& BSEG-HKONT=’account entered’

is there possible with adaption Project?

the other requirement is to

2. add additional columns within the standard, what is the kind of project to make that?

a.create an adaptation project?

b.copy the existing project with program /UI5/UI5_REPOSITORY_LOAD, executing from SE38

in the backend

a. what is the step by step for making, i need to copy the Odata in SEGW or the CDS View and publish in Eclipse?

 

 

Thanks 

SAP BTP, ABAP environment SAPUI5

entry.title: Re: How to update the custom fields added via key user ex...
entry.content:
Hi TG1,

I am unfortunately not an expert on Billing but I checked available APIs that are enabled for custom fields- I don't see they do update (see Read and Create). I don't see an app that does mass change of billing doc (besides mass update of output items)

Could you provide us some more info about your requirement?

Does it need to be on the Billing Doc? as you mention the info is available only on goods receipt, could you update the delivery? I know there are APIs available to update the delivery custom field after the goods receipt.

Will all the documents have the same value for the custom field?

is it something that could be sent with the GR, can you do the GR via API?

Thank you

Jerry

entry.title: Re: Cannot select destination for live data run configura...
entry.content:
Hi @rsham_ososoft 
our SAP experts analyzed your case.

In the exercise on "Consuming an External Service from SAP S/4HANA", there is a "Task 4: Configure an SAP Business Accelerator Hub Destination". This was either not done or not done correctly. 
Please check from your side if the task was performed at all.
If so, it would be great if we could get a screenshot.
That way, we can more easily what went wrong and provide a solution.
Thanks and best regards
Margit

entry.content: Hello, I have exactly the same problem. How was it finally solved? Best regards

entry.title: What type of project or enhancement should I undertake to add columns to F3302 In BAS?
entry.content:
BAS

Version: 1.87.1
Commit: 
Date: 2024-04-04T07:11:26.010Z
Browser: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/124.0.0.0 Safari/537.36

I was working on customizing SAP Business Application Studio and implementing some specific logic and modifications.

 

i dont know if i need to create an Adaptation Project in SAP Business Application Studio and then implement the extension using Visual Editor or create an other option when created the project.

  1. Filtering Account Assignment Category:

    • I want to implement logic based on conditions like BSEG-BLART='WE' or BSEG-BLART='WA', BSEG-BSCHL≠'96', and BSEG-HKONT='account entered'.
    • it's possible to implement this logic using an adaptation project. I can utilize the Visual Editor to define these filter conditions based on the fields you've mentioned, how can i Add this login in the new filter?

2. Adding Additional Columns:

  • To add additional columns within the standard, I have a couple of options:
    • Create an adaptation project: This would involve extending the existing application with additional fields in the table or modifying the existing ones.
    • Copy the existing project with program /UI5/UI5_REPOSITORY_LOAD: This approach involves copying the existing project and then making modifications as needed. This can be more complex but gives you greater control over the changes.

  1. Regarding the backend steps:

    • For creating an adaptation project:

      • If you're adding fields that require changes in the backend, you might need to extend the existing OData service or create a new one using SAP Gateway Service Builder (transaction SEGW).
      • After modifying the OData service, you'll need to publish the changes in Eclipse.

    • For copying the existing project:

      • If the modifications require changes in the backend, you'll likely need to copy the relevant CDS views and/or OData services and then adjust them as necessary.
      • After making changes, you'll also need to publish the modified artifacts in Eclipse.

 

what is the step by step

I downloaded and uploaded the application, modified the annotations and the manifest, and encountered the following error. Is this the right path?

are there any blog that help me to make the rigth decision and the step?


Thanks

entry.title: Making forms available to an external company or person
entry.content:
I've setup a form as a trigger in a process, with approvals being sent to my SAP Build Inbox.

All good, the form is accessed via a public url, which is accessible if logged into BTP and the approval comes through fine.

My question is: What is the process for sending a from to an external company/user (without BTP access)?

entry.title: Re: In RMK when a candidate accepts the online offer, the...
entry.content: The verbiage can be updated via Manage Languages: https://me.sap.com/notes/2576546/E
